item.title: QVS to defend Lutubula Trophy against RKS in Deans Eastern Zone semi-finals
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 15/07/2026 Defending Lutubula Trophy champions Queen Victoria School (QVS) will face Ratu Kadavulevu School (RKS) in the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U18 semi-finals this weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 15/07/2026

Defending Lutubula Trophy champions Queen Victoria School (QVS) will face Ratu Kadavulevu School (RKS) in the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U18 semi-finals this weekend.

The Lutubula Trophy, once the hallmark of dominance among Eastern Zone rugby powerhouses such as QVS, RKS and Lelean Memorial School, forms part of the qualification pathway for schools aiming to reach the Vodafone Deans competition.

Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union Association Secretary Epeli Kalou says if QVS loses to RKS this weekend, a new Lutubula Trophy holder will be crowned in the final. However, if QVS successfully defends the trophy, another challenge will be held during next week's final.

Kalou says Lelean Memorial School is the favourite in the U18 grade, having remained unbeaten throughout the competition with victories over both RKS and QVS.

He adds that it will be an exciting weekend of rugby, with both QVS and RKS determined to avoid dropping into the elimination bracket.

Kalou also says this week's semi-finals will be played at St Vincent College Ground in Natovi.

He says the venue was chosen to help reduce travel expenses for St John's College while also providing a neutral venue for all four semi-finalists.

The U18 semi-finals will see Ratu Kadavulevu School take on Queen Victoria School, while Lelean Memorial School will face St John's College.

item.title: Spain beat France 2-0 to reach the FIFA World Cup final after 16 years
item.description: After 16 years Spain are back in the FIFA World Cup final after defeating France 2-0 in the first semi-final. Mikel Oyarzabal opened the scoring from the penalty spot in the 22nd minute before Pedro Porro doubled Spain’s lead in the 58th minute, with Dani Olmo providing the assist.
item.content:encoded:
After 16 years Spain are back in the FIFA World Cup final after defeating France 2-0 in the first semi-final.

Mikel Oyarzabal opened the scoring from the penalty spot in the 22nd minute before Pedro Porro doubled Spain’s lead in the 58th minute, with Dani Olmo providing the assist.

French fans were left heartbroken as their team's hopes of reaching the World Cup final came to an end.

Spain will face the winner of the second semi-final between England and Argentina, which kicks off at 7am tomorrow , while France will play in the third-place play-off.

The third-place play-off will be played at 9am on Sunday.

Meanwhile, the FIFA World Cup final will be played at 7am on Monday.

item.title: Lekutu Secondary girls chase final after historic semi-final qualification
item.description: Nilufa Buksh , Priya Nand · 15/07/2026 Lekutu Secondary School has created history by qualifying for the semi-finals of the Fiji Secondary Schools competition for the first time, and are currently taking on Labasa College for a place in the final.
item.content:encoded:
Nilufa Buksh , Priya Nand · 15/07/2026

Lekutu Secondary School has created history by qualifying for the semi-finals of the Fiji Secondary Schools competition for the first time, and are currently taking on Labasa College for a place in the final.

Geography Teacher and Coach Divendra Prasad says the achievement is a testament to the hard work and dedication of the players, teachers and coaching staff despite limited preparation time.

Prasad says the team faced tough opposition throughout the pool matches and picked up a few injuries along the way, but the girls remained determined and managed to overcome some strong teams to secure a place in the last four.

He says the team only had two days to prepare for the semi-final because students had to return home after training each day.

Prasad says balancing studies and sports has not been easy, but the players have managed both well, with some also performing strongly in their examinations.

He says the team has full confidence in the girls and believes they are capable of beating Labasa College to secure a place in the final.

Meanwhile, Waidele College will take on Dreketi Central College 12pm today at Labasa Muslim Grounds.

item.title: Northern Bulls hold off Northland in 27-25 Ranadi Cup Round 2 clash
item.description: Northern Bulls captain Laisani Salawaqa has praised her side's teamwork after they secured a hard-fought 27-25 victory over Northland in Round 2 of the Ranadi Cup at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori. The narrow win saw the Northern Bulls hold off a determined Northland side in one of the closest matches of the round.
item.content:encoded:
Northern Bulls captain Laisani Salawaqa has praised her side's teamwork after they secured a hard-fought 27-25 victory over Northland in Round 2 of the Ranadi Cup at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori.

The narrow win saw the Northern Bulls hold off a determined Northland side in one of the closest matches of the round.

Speaking after the match, Salawaqa thanked her players for their effort and acknowledged the challenge posed by their opponents.

Salawaqa says the victory has given the team confidence as they turn their attention to their next fixture.

The Northern Bulls will now shift their focus to Round 3 against Ra at 11am on Saturday at the Narauyaba Sports Ground.

Other Round 2 Ranadi Cup results saw some dominant performances, with Cakaudrove securing the biggest win of the round after defeating Bua 86-12.

Yasawa also recorded a convincing victory, beating Tavua 59-7, while Ra overcame Coastland 42-5.

Navosa continued their strong form with a 63-32 win over Malolo, while Serua claimed a 57-29 victory against Ovalau.

item.title: Four Marist teams eye Southern Zone rugby finals berths
item.description: Marist Brothers High School will feature in four grades during the Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary School Rugby Union semi-finals this week, with its Under-15, Under-16, Under-17, and Under-18 sides all aiming to secure places in their respective finals and also the national finals. The school's Under-14 campaign came to an end after a 19-10 quarterfinal defeat to Suva Grammar School last w
item.content:encoded:
Marist Brothers High School will feature in four grades during the Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary School Rugby Union semi-finals this week, with its Under-15, Under-16, Under-17, and Under-18 sides all aiming to secure places in their respective finals and also the national finals.

The school's Under-14 campaign came to an end after a 19-10 quarterfinal defeat to Suva Grammar School last week.

The Under-15 side progressed to the semi-finals with an impressive 40-22 victory over Ratu Simione Matanitobua College while their Under-16 grade produced a dominant performance, defeating Dudley High School 31-6.

The Under-17 team booked its semi-final spot with a convincing 29-10 win over Ratu Latianara College.

In the Under-18 grade, Marist advanced to the semifinals and will await confirmation of its opponent when the semifinal fixtures are announced.

The Southern Zone semi-finals and finals will be held at the Grammar Grounds in Suva.

item.title: Tanya Kalounivale: Queensland Reds and Black Ferns prop ready for Fiji Drua women's.
item.description: Former Suva Grammar School student Tanya Kalounivale is ready to put on the Queensland Reds jersey as they prepare to take on the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side in the Super Rugby Women semi-finals this Saturday. Kalounivale was born in Fiji and later moved to New Zealand after securing a contract to play for Waikato.
item.content:encoded:
Former Suva Grammar School student Tanya Kalounivale is ready to put on the Queensland Reds jersey as they prepare to take on the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side in the Super Rugby Women semi-finals this Saturday.

Kalounivale was born in Fiji and later moved to New Zealand after securing a contract to play for Waikato.

The tighthead prop made her debut for Waikato in 2017, building her into one of the premier props in women's rugby.

Kalounivale made her Test debut for New Zealand's Black Ferns in 2022 and quickly established herself as a dominant scrummager and ball carrier.

Sh e was a member of the Black Ferns squad that won the 2022 Rugby World Cup and went on to represent New Zealand at a second Rugby World Cup, earning 29 Test caps during her international career.

This year, Kalounivale signed with the Queensland Reds for the Super Rugby Women's competition.

The move brought her closer to family in Brisbane while providing a new challenge in Australian rugby.

Her experience, physicality and leadership have added significant strength to the Reds' forward pack, where she forms one of the competition's strongest front-row combinations.

Known for her powerful scrummaging, aggressive defence and relentless work rate, Kalounivale continues to be a major influence on and off the field.

Her journey from Fiji to international rugby and now to the Queensland Reds reflects her dedication, resilience and commitment to excellence in the sport.

The Reds will face the Fiji Drua at 1pm at Churchill Park, Lautoka.

item.description: Cakaudrove captain Finau Kaulele says discipline and respect for referees will be key as the team sets its sights on winning this

item.title: Too early to judge Seruvakula after just two Tests - Rabuka
item.description: Two Test matches is not enough to gauge whether having a local coach has been succesful or not.Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka stat
